title: Prague 2-Day City Break
metaTitle: Prague 2-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Prague, Czechia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Prague Castle complex and St. Vitus Cathedral (crypt & tower). Prices from $889 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Prague, Czechia — Continental climate with cold winters (highs 2°C, occasional snow) and warm summers (highs 24°C); spring and autumn are mild and pleasant. April to May and September to October offer mild weather and thinner crowds than the packed summer months. This itinerary blends Prague Castle complex, St. Vitus Cathedral (crypt & tower), Charles Bridge with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Prague Castle complex, St. Vitus Cathedral (crypt & tower), Charles Bridge
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Prague
itinerary.description: Land at Václav Havel Airport Prague (PRG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Prague Castle complex — Largest ancient castle complex in the world. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Prague Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to St. Vitus Cathedral (crypt & tower) — Gothic cathedral within Prague Castle grounds. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to PRG for your onward flight.

travelTips: Buy a 24 or 72-hour transit pass if planning to use trams/metro frequently.
Climb Petřín or Vyšehrad instead of the crowded Castle viewpoint for similar views with fewer people.
Czech beer is famously cheap and high-quality — often less than bottled water at restaurants.
Exchange currency at bank-affiliated kiosks, not the "0% commission" storefronts near Old Town Square which use poor rates.
Visit Prague Castle early morning to beat tour bus crowds.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Comfortable shoes for cobblestone streets and hills
A warm coat for winter (highs near freezing)
thingsToAvoid: Currency exchange booths advertising "0% commission" near tourist zones — rates are typically terrible.
Restaurants near Old Town Square with unmarked prices or no printed menu — check the bill for added charges.
Overpriced "traditional" Trdelník pastry stalls, which are actually more Slovak/Hungarian in origin.
Taxis hailed on the street without a metered app — use Bolt or Uber instead.
Walking on tram tracks or crossing without checking both directions.
topRestaurants: La Degustation Bohême Bourgeoise — Fine Dining Czech Tasting Menu ($$$$)
adventureActivities: Day trip caving/hiking in Bohemian Switzerland National Park
luxuryExperiences: Czech crystal shopping with a personal consultant
honeymoonExperiences: Private candlelit dinner overlooking the castle
Couples spa with a Czech beer-bath experience
budgetExperiences: Free walk across Charles Bridge and Old Town Square
Explore the Lennon Wall and Petřín gardens for free
Cheap, excellent Czech beer at neighborhood pubs
Vyšehrad park for free castle-adjacent views
localEtiquette: Greet with "Dobrý den" when entering shops or restaurants.
Tipping around 10% is customary in restaurants; round up for smaller purchases.
Remove shoes when entering a Czech home, if invited.
Avoid loud, rowdy behavior — Prague has cracked down on stag/hen party excesses in the historic center.
Don't jaywalk across tram tracks — trams have priority and stop distances are long.
localTransportGuide: Prague's integrated metro, tram and bus network runs on a single ticket system — validate paper tickets or use a contactless card tap. Trams are especially useful for reaching the castle district, and the historic center itself is very walkable.
bestTimeToVisit: April to May and September to October offer mild weather and thinner crowds than the packed summer months.
weather: Continental climate with cold winters (highs 2°C, occasional snow) and warm summers (highs 24°C); spring and autumn are mild and pleasant.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Prague?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Prague?
faqs.answer: April to May and September to October offer mild weather and thinner crowds than the packed summer months. Continental climate with cold winters (highs 2°C, occasional snow) and warm summers (highs 24°C); spring and autumn are mild and pleasant.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Prague safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Prague is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Prague?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CZK. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Yuki van Dijk
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: National Theatre performance alone was worth the trip, but combined with Petřín Tower this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Smith
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Prague is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Wenceslas Square. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Emma Sharma
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Prague was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Astronomical Clock tower climb was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Costa
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Beer spa experience and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Daniel Smith
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to St. Vitus Cathedral (crypt & tower) and Vyšehrad Casemates tour. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Liam Andersen
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Our trip to Prague was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Jewish Museum & Old Jewish Cemetery was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
